title: Login anomaly detection — impossible travel + new device (negative pack login monitoring)
business_reason: Beyond brute-force, the same identity authenticating from many distinct IPs (impossible travel) or many devices (new device) is a credential-compromise signal. Extends the suspicious-login scanner.

design.scan: PURE scanLoginAnomalies(events) — groups SUCCESSFUL logins by identity; >=2 distinct IPs -> impossible_travel, >=2 distinct device fingerprints -> new_device; severity escalates at 4+. Unit-tested (5).
design.runner: runSuspiciousLoginScan now loads login_failed AND login_success (with device_fingerprint) and raises brute_force + impossible_travel + new_device alerts (idempotent), incident on high/critical. Shared by route + sweep.
design.ui: surfaced via the existing security-console suspicious-login panel + alerts.

param_14.question: Do we detect compromised-credential signals beyond brute force?
param_14.answer: Yes. The login scan also flags impossible travel (one identity from multiple distinct IPs) and new devices (multiple device fingerprints), raising idempotent alerts with escalating severity and an incident at high risk — on-demand and in the daily sweep.
param_14.security_implications: detects account takeover / shared-credential patterns; idempotent alerts; high -> incident; runs unattended via the sweep.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: geo-IP distance for true impossible-travel; device-trust registry; notify-on-alert.
